name: vsxsentry-guard
description: Auto-block and remove malicious / risky VS Code extensions using the VSXSentry feeds.

vsxSentry.malicious.feedUrl.description: CSV feed of malicious extensions. Always enforced silently.
vsxSentry.malicious.feedUrl.markdownDescription:
CSV feed of malicious extensions. Always enforced silently.

[View source code on GitHub](https://github.com/vsxsentry/vsxsentry-guard) | [Report an issue](https://github.com/vsxsentry/vsxsentry-guard/issues)

vsxSentry.risky.enabled.description: Also enforce the risky feed. When enabled, you'll be prompted before action - risky entries are often legitimate tools.

vsxSentry.risky.severities.description: Severities from the risky feed to act on.

vsxSentry.risky.categories.description: Categories to act on (e.g. 'risky-tunnel', 'risky-remote-access', 'risky-ai-code-access'). Use ['*'] for all.

vsxSentry.action.description: What to do with detected installed extensions. 'remove' uninstalls; 'disable' just disables; 'warn' only notifies.

vsxSentry.applyToAllowedSetting.description: Also write blocked IDs to extensions.allowed so VS Code refuses future installs (subject to existing user/policy config).

vsxSentry.updateIntervalHours.description: How often to re-fetch the feeds in the background.

vsxSentry.notifyOnNewEntries.description: Show a notification when the feeds gain new entries since last check.
